lede: A look at July 2 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Larry David and Hermann Hesse, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Sivas
events.desc: A mob sets fire to the Hotel Madımak in Sivas, Turkey, where a Alevi cultural festival was taking place, killing 37 people.

events.title: Civil rights movement
events.desc: Civil rights movement: U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson signs the Civil Rights Act of 1964 meant to prohibit segregation in public places.

events.title: Amelia Earhart
events.desc: Amelia Earhart and navigator Fred Noonan are last heard from over the Pacific Ocean and disappear while attempting to make the first equatorial round-the-world flight.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: U.S. President Warren G. Harding signs the Knox–Porter Resolution formally ending the war between the United States and Germany.

events.title: United States Congress
events.desc: The U.S. Congress passes the Sherman Antitrust Act.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: On the second day of the Battle of Gettysburg - the Battle of Little Round Top takes place and results in a Union victory after the Confederate troops unsuccessfully try to assault the Union left flank.

events.title: Bahia
events.desc: Bahia Independence Day: The Siege of Salvador ends Portuguese rule in Brazil, with the final defeat of the Portuguese crown loyalists in the province of Bahia.

events.title: English Civil War
events.desc: English Civil War: Battle of Marston Moor.

events.title: Battle of Yamazaki
events.desc: Battle of Yamazaki: Toyotomi Hideyoshi defeats Akechi Mitsuhide.

events.title: Emperor Taizong of Tang
events.desc: Li Shimin, the future Emperor Taizong of Tang, ambushes and kills his rival brothers Li Yuanji and Li Jiancheng in the Xuanwu Gate Incident.

births.name: Larry David
births.line: American actor, comedian, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Hermann Hesse
births.line: German-born Swiss poet, novelist, and painter, Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Wisława Szymborska
births.line: Polish poet and translator, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Elie Wiesel, Holocaust survivor, activist, and author
Ernest Hemingway, American novelist, short story writer, and journalist, Nobel Prize laureate

observances.text: Christian feast day: Aberoh and Atom (Coptic Church)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Visitation (Anglicanism; Levoča at Mariánska hora)
